articles.summary: The Supreme Court has initiated suo motu proceedings to address the high costs of essential life-saving drugs. The move follows a petition highlighting the plight of patients who cannot afford critical medications. The court is seeking a report from a Working Group to evaluate pricing structures and ensure the fundamental right to health is protected for all citizens.

articles.headline: Four students and cyclist killed as train rams car in Murshidabad
articles.summary: A tragic accident occurred at a railway gate near Karnasubarna station when a passenger train rammed into a school pool car. Four children aged seven to nine and a 65-year-old cyclist were killed in the collision. The driver of the pool car has been hospitalized and the gate-man has been arrested for negligence in failing to close the crossing gate in time.

articles.headline: IMD issues landslide alerts for Western Ghats and Coastal Karnataka
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department has issued orange alerts for heavy rainfall in coastal and Malnad districts for the next two days. Authorities have warned of an increased risk of landslides across the Western Ghats and coastal Karnataka. Residents in low-lying areas have been advised to remain alert as a trough extending from West Bay of Bengal brings intense precipitation.

articles.headline: Ukrainian forces strike major oil refinery in Russia's Yaroslavl region
articles.summary: A Ukrainian drone strike targeted a major oil refinery in Yaroslavl, northeast of Moscow. The facility, which has a capacity of 300,000 barrels a day, was hit as part of a series of targeted attacks on Russian energy infrastructure. Local authorities confirmed the strike but did not provide immediate details on casualties or the extent of long-term operational damage.

articles.headline: Metro commuters face Rs 2,500 fine for loud music on speakers
articles.summary: The Bengaluru Metro Rail Corporation Limited (BMRCL) has enhanced fines for nuisance on metro premises. Playing loud music on speakers, watching videos without earphones, or creating disturbances now attracts a penalty of up to Rs 2,500, up from Rs 500. Officials stated that security personnel have warned nearly 57,000 passengers during 2025-26 for audio-related violations.

articles.headline: Community revives century-old British-era well in Bengaluru's Cox Town
articles.summary: Residents of Cox Town have successfully restored a neglected British-era well that had been filled with silt and sewage for decades. The initiative, led by local volunteers and NGOs, has transformed the site into a sustainable local water source. The well is 45 feet deep and can now store up to 81 kiloliters of water, providing a vital resource for the neighborhood's community gardens.

articles.headline: Hoax bomb note at KIA sparks security scare and flight delays
articles.summary: A handwritten hoax bomb note found on an Ahmedabad-bound flight at Kempegowda International Airport (KIA) triggered a major security alert on Thursday night. The aircraft was moved to an isolated bay where bomb squads and sniffer dogs conducted a two-hour search. No suspicious items were found, and the flight was eventually cleared for takeoff at 1:20 AM on Friday.

articles.headline: North corporation clears 86 blackspots since mid-June in cleanliness drive
articles.summary: The Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP) has cleared 86 of 100 identified chronic garbage dumping 'blackspots' in the North zone. The 'U-100' initiative involves intensive cleaning, beautification, and night vigils to prevent re-dumping. Officials are also identifying residents who refuse to hand over waste to door-to-door collectors, imposing fines to ensure compliance.

articles.headline: Why Karnataka and Centre are locked in a 14,300-crore land battle
articles.summary: A dispute has intensified between the Karnataka government and the Centre over 430 acres of land in Bengaluru belonging to HMT Ltd. The state government moved to reclaim the land, valued at Rs 14,300 crore, as forest area, following a 1901 notification. The Centre and HMT argue the land was lawfully transferred for industrial use and are challenging the state's move in court.

articles.headline: Panel to be formed to fix technical bottlenecks in e-Swathu
articles.summary: The Karnataka government will constitute a five-member committee to resolve technical issues in the e-Swathu (e-khata) process for rural property owners. The system, intended to streamline property registration, has faced significant hurdles, leaving lakhs of applications pending. The committee is expected to submit its recommendations within 10 days to facilitate smoother transactions.

articles.headline: NFHS data shows spike in C-section births driven by private hospitals
articles.summary: National Family Health Survey (NFHS-6) data reveals that C-section deliveries in Karnataka have risen to 45.7% of all births. The spike is predominantly driven by the private sector, where 63.8% of births are surgical, compared to 34% in public health facilities. Health officials expressed concern over the rising trend and the potential long-term maternal and neonatal health implications.

articles.headline: Sanjay Gandhi Institute launches first robotic surgery system for joint replacement
articles.summary: The Sanjay Gandhi Institute of Trauma and Orthopaedics (SGITO) has become the first government hospital in Karnataka to introduce a Rs 3-crore robotic surgery system for joint replacements. The technology is expected to improve precision in knee and hip surgeries, reducing blood loss and enabling faster patient recovery times. Plans are underway to expand this technology to other state hospitals.

articles.headline: Coca-Cola wins 45 lakh in e-waste GST dispute with recycler
articles.summary: A Bengaluru commercial court has ordered an e-waste recycler to pay Rs 45.2 lakh to Coca-Cola over a GST miscalculation. The recycler had wrongly charged 18% GST instead of the statutory 5% for scrap disposal services. Despite an agreement to credit the excess amount, the recycler failed to do so, leading to the legal victory for the beverage giant.

articles.headline: Landslide risks return to Karnataka as heavy monsoon rains resume
articles.summary: After a two-week lull, heavy monsoon rains have returned to Karnataka, causing a landslide near a school in Kumta. The IMD has issued alerts for several coastal and Malnad districts. While the rains have brought relief to Bengaluru's temperatures, the high intensity in the Ghats has prompted district administrations to remain on high alert for further landslide and flooding incidents.

articles.headline: HC issues notice to government on power connection relief for unauthorized buildings
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has issued notices to the state government and ESCOMs regarding a PIL challenging the grant of permanent electricity connections to buildings without occupancy certificates. The government had recently issued an order allowing one-time exemptions for such structures. The petitioner argues the move legitimizes illegal buildings and violates earlier Supreme Court directions.

articles.headline: Meerut engineers masterminded 357 crore income tax refund fraud
articles.summary: Income tax officials have uncovered a massive Rs 357 crore refund fraud orchestrated by a 30-year-old woman and her accomplices in Meerut. The group used a network to file 3,000 fraudulent tax returns for clients across India by exploiting system loopholes. Raids conducted at their premises led to the seizure of cash and digital evidence detailing the multi-crore racket.

articles.headline: Mobile internet cut in J&K district following tense police killing incident
articles.summary: Authorities in Jammu and Kashmir have suspended mobile internet services in Doda district following a tense situation sparked by the police killing of a man on Thursday. Local protests erupted after the incident, leading to heavy deployment of security forces. The administration stated the internet cut was a precautionary measure to prevent the spread of rumors and maintain law and order.

articles.headline: 45 million Indian graduates are neither working nor looking for work
articles.summary: A new statistical report indicates that 45 million Indian graduates have opted out of the labor force entirely. Despite holding higher education degrees, over 60% of women in this cohort are not seeking employment. Only 10% of the unemployed are actively looking for jobs, highlighting a significant challenge in labor market participation and gender disparity in the professional workforce.

articles.headline: Department of Space staff vacancy rate hits 25-year high of 30%
articles.summary: The Department of Space (DoS) is facing a critical staffing crisis with vacancy rates hitting a 25-year high of nearly 30%. Only 14,637 employees are currently in place against a sanctioned strength of 20,269. The department attributed the shortfall to a wave of retirements and a pause in recruitment during the COVID-19 pandemic, raising concerns about future mission timelines.

articles.headline: Reliance Industries Q1 profit plunges 25% despite 25% revenue growth
articles.summary: Reliance Industries (RIL) reported a 25.3% drop in its first-quarter consolidated net profit to Rs 23,196 crore. The decline was attributed to higher operational costs and a one-off gain in the previous year. However, revenue grew by 25% to Rs 2.1 lakh crore, driven by strong performances in the retail and telecommunications segments. The company remains optimistic about future energy projects.

articles.headline: Smartphone sales in India decline 10% in biggest dip in 3 years
articles.summary: India's smartphone market saw a 10% year-on-year decline in shipments during the first quarter, the largest drop in three years. The slump was most pronounced in the sub-Rs 15,000 segment, which saw a 45% plunge. High inventory levels and a shift in consumer preference toward premium devices contributed to the decline, while Chinese brands saw their combined market share shrink to its lowest level.

articles.headline: Sensex soars 965 points as Q1 results lift market sentiment
articles.summary: The BSE Sensex surged by 965 points to close at 24,334 on Friday, buoyed by positive Q1 earnings from blue-chip companies including RIL and Tech Mahindra. The Nifty also gained 0.5% to finish at 78,152. Market analysts attributed the rally to strong domestic institutional buying and a positive outlook for the banking and tech sectors despite global economic headwinds.

articles.headline: Former India U-19 cricketer Siddharth Kalra arrested for spot-fixing in Sri Lanka
articles.summary: Siddharth Kalra, a former India U-19 World Cup player and co-owner of a Lanka Premier League team, has been arrested in Colombo on charges of spot-fixing. Kalra is accused of attempting to bribe five players during the tournament. Four other Sri Lankan players are also under investigation by the Special Investigation Unit for sports-related crimes.

articles.headline: Pele's 1958 World Cup final jersey sells for record 4.9 million dollars
articles.summary: A jersey worn by Brazilian football legend Pele during the 1958 World Cup final has been sold for a record $4.9 million at a Sotheby's auction. The jersey, which had been in a private collection for decades, is now the second-most valuable piece of sports memorabilia ever sold. Pele had originally gifted the jersey to a teammate after Brazil's historic 5-2 victory over Sweden.

articles.headline: NMC moves to allow for-profit medical colleges in landmark policy shift
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has proposed draft regulations to allow for-profit companies to establish medical colleges. Currently, only non-profit trusts or societies are permitted to run such institutions. The move aims to increase the number of medical seats in India by attracting more private investment, though critics worry it may lead to even higher medical education costs.

articles.headline: Gang steals 16th-century cannon weighing one tonne from Madhya Pradesh fort
articles.summary: An armed gang of 25 to 30 men raided the ASI-protected Narwar Fort in Madhya Pradesh and stole a rare 16th-century bronze cannon. The massive weapon, weighing over 1,000 kg, was taken after the gang overpowered security guards. Police suspect a professional network involved in the illegal trade of antique artifacts, as the theft required heavy machinery and transport.

articles.headline: Tata Memorial scrutinizes study claiming papaya leaf extract ups platelet count
articles.summary: The Tata Memorial Hospital has placed a peer-reviewed study on papaya leaf extract under scrutiny following concerns about its methodology. The study claimed the extract could significantly increase platelet counts in chemotherapy patients. However, independent health experts have questioned the findings, prompting the hospital to conduct an internal review of the research data.

articles.headline: US forces strike Iranian Chabahar port amid regional tensions
articles.summary: The US military conducted airstrikes targeting bridge and power infrastructure in Iran, including a surveillance tower at the strategic Chabahar port. Iran retaliated with missile strikes against US allies in the Persian Gulf. Indian-operated interests at Chabahar are reportedly affected by the repeated strikes, though Iran has not officially confirmed the full extent of the tower damage.

articles.headline: Andy Burnham becomes Labour leader and seventh UK Prime Minister
articles.summary: Andy Burnham has been elected as the new leader of the UK Labour Party and will become the country's seventh Prime Minister in a decade. Pledging to improve living standards, Burnham plans to devolve power to regions and set up a 'Number 10 North'. He criticized previous Conservative policies for leaving essential services like housing and energy exposed to high costs.

articles.headline: Five killed including four students as train hits vehicle in WB
articles.summary: Four school students were among five people killed when a passenger train hit a pool car at a level crossing in West Bengal's Murshidabad. Officials stated the gateman incorrectly opened the gate to allow vehicles through while a train was approaching. The gateman has been arrested, and a permanent way inspector has been suspended pending an inquiry into the safety lapse.

articles.headline: Apple briefly unseats Nvidia as most valuable firm globally
articles.summary: Apple briefly reclaimed its crown as the world’s most valuable public company, overtaking Nvidia amid a sell-off in semiconductor shares. Nvidia’s stock fell by 4% as investors questioned the long-term sustainability of the AI boom. Apple’s market valuation touched $4.8 trillion, slightly ahead of Nvidia’s $4.6 trillion, reflecting volatility in the technology sector.

articles.headline: Japan dismisses ex-minister's comments on bullet train project as incorrect
articles.summary: The Indian government has dismissed comments by a former Japanese minister regarding the lack of progress on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project as 'factually incorrect.' Officials stated that work is progressing rapidly, with the first section expected to open in 2027. Japan remains a key partner, and the project has moved into the heavy civil engineering and track-laying phases.

articles.headline: Supreme Court orders Greater Bengaluru Authority polls before December 31
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has set a firm deadline of December 31, 2026, for conducting elections to 369 wards across five city corporations under the GBA. The court granted a final extension to the state government, which had requested time until 2027. Deputy CM DK Shivakumar assured the court that the government is committed to completing the delimitation and poll process by the year-end.

articles.headline: PM flags off India's first hydrogen-powered train in Haryana
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated India’s first indigenously developed hydrogen-powered train from Jind in Haryana. The launch represents a significant step toward achieving carbon-neutral railways. Modi stated that the move would reduce dependence on imported fossil fuels and provide a cleaner mode of transport, positioning India as a global leader in green hydrogen technology.

articles.headline: Trump accuses China of 2020 election interference and voter data theft
articles.summary: In a televised speech, Donald Trump accused China of interfering in the 2020 US election and stealing data from 220 million voters. China has denied the allegations, calling them 'groundless accusations.' The claims come as the US administration moves to further restrict visas for Chinese journalists and increase scrutiny on foreign influence in upcoming election cycles.

articles.headline: CDSCO approves Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy for fatty liver treatment
articles.summary: India's drugs regulator, CDSCO, has approved Novo Nordisk's semaglutide injection (Wegovy) for the treatment of non-cirrhotic non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH). This is the first glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist approved in India for advanced liver fibrosis. The approval offers a new treatment option for a disease caused by excess fat build-up in the liver.

articles.headline: Cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers passes away at age 88
articles.summary: The sporting world mourns the death of West Indies legend Sir Garfield Sobers, widely regarded as the greatest all-rounder in cricket history. Sobers, who was the first player to hit six sixes in a first-class over, died on Friday. He played 93 Tests, scoring 8,032 runs and taking 235 wickets. Tributes have poured in from global icons including Sunil Gavaskar and Sachin Tendulkar.

articles.headline: FIFA World Cup final drives massive business for Indian hospitality sector
articles.summary: The upcoming FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain has led to a surge in restaurant and multiplex bookings. The NRAI expects business to double in cities like Bengaluru and Hyderabad due to extended night hours. Multiplexes like PVR Inox have added over 100 screens in 53 cities, reporting occupancy rates as high as 90% for late-night screenings.

articles.headline: Reliance Industries reports quarterly net profit of 20,946 crore rupees
articles.summary: Reliance Industries Limited (RIL) posted a consolidated net profit of 20,946 crore for the first quarter, exceeding market expectations. Despite a 22.4% year-on-year decline in profit, revenue from its O2C and telecom segments saw significant growth. Chairman Mukesh Ambani noted strong contributions from digital services and the telecommunications business to the overall performance.

articles.headline: Gujarat, Maharashtra, TN Top Niti Index
articles.summary: Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu have been ranked as the top three states in Niti Aayog’s Investment Friendliness Index. The ranking evaluates states on infrastructure, ease of doing business, and policy stability. Other states like Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, and Andhra Pradesh also showed significant improvements, reflecting a nationwide push to attract more domestic and foreign investment.

articles.headline: Reliance Retail net profit drops 14 percent despite revenue growth
articles.summary: Reliance Retail Ventures reported a 14.1% year-on-year decline in net profit to 2,805 crore for the first quarter of FY27. While daily revenue grew 12% to 45,961 crore, profitability was impacted by higher finance costs which rose 34% annually. Executive Director Isha Ambani highlighted the steady growth in the company's digital commerce and omni-channel capabilities.

articles.headline: JSW Steel consolidated net profit more than doubles in first quarter
articles.summary: JSW Steel reported a 113% surge in consolidated net profit to 4,651 crore for the first quarter of FY27. Performance was bolstered by improved steel prices, higher sales volumes, and a better product mix. Total revenue reached 47,364 crore, exceeding market estimates despite fluctuations in raw material costs and maintenance-related shutdowns at its Vijayanagar plant.

articles.headline: Federal Bank net profit increases 37 percent to 1,177 crore
articles.summary: Federal Bank posted a 37% year-on-year increase in net profit for the June quarter, driven by strong loan growth and improved interest margins. Net interest income rose 26% to 2,946 crore. Asset quality improved, with gross non-performing assets falling to 1.52% of total advances. The bank benefited from a sharp reduction in funding costs compared to asset yields.

articles.headline: Central Bank of India reports 13 percent rise in net profit
articles.summary: State-owned Central Bank of India recorded a net profit of 1,324 crore for the first quarter of FY27, up 13% from 1,169 crore last year. Total income increased to 10,678 crore. The bank's asset quality improved significantly as gross non-performing assets (NPA) dropped to 2.60% from 3.13% year-on-year, while net interest income showed positive growth.

articles.headline: IAMAI establishes E-commerce Council of India to boost digital trade
articles.summary: The Internet and Mobile Association of India (IAMAI) launched the E-commerce Council of India (ECCI) to foster collaboration between industry and government. Over 70 members, including Amazon, Flipkart, and Tata 1mg, have joined. The council aims to develop best practices and contribute to policy frameworks for digital commerce, logistics, and supply chain infrastructure.

articles.headline: Anand Mahindra says AI will enhance rather than replace IT services
articles.summary: Tech Mahindra Chairman Anand Mahindra told shareholders that Artificial Intelligence (AI) will not eliminate the IT services sector. Instead, AI will make the industry more vital by providing the tools to turn data into business intelligence. He compared the AI era to the smartphone revolution, asserting that IT firms will remain essential partners in enterprise intelligence.

articles.headline: Xi Jinping Urges International AI Cooperation Amid Rising Security Concerns
articles.summary: President Xi Jinping called for global collaboration on artificial intelligence rules at the World AI Conference in Shanghai. Emphasizing safety and equality, Xi's debut at the event highlights China's ambition to shape technology governance. The move comes as Chinese AI startups release models rivaling top U.S. systems, while both nations weigh restrictions on foreign access to tech.

articles.headline: Manipal Hospitals Slashes IPO Valuation to $8.3 b
articles.summary: Manipal Health Enterprises has reduced the valuation for its upcoming IPO to approximately $8.3 billion, down from an earlier target. The decision reflects a more cautious approach amidst current market volatility and investor feedback. Despite the lower valuation, the hospital chain still plans to raise significant funds for expansion and debt reduction.
